description: Hand-curated seed set of reference strategies for mangrove-agent. Populated by copying known-good configs from MangroveAI's reference_strategies table + TA best practices from the KB. Replaced by live mangroveai.reference_strategies.search() once MangroveOracle issue #156 + MangroveAI endpoint land. Until then, the agent picks from these via /create-strategy skill (Mechanism 2). Signal names verified against MangroveAI/src/MangroveAI/domains/signals/signals_metadata.json (2026-04-22 snapshot).

strategies.label: ETH momentum — MACD bullish cross on 1h
strategies.description: Enters on a MACD bullish cross, exits on bearish cross. MACD(12,26,9) is the canonical setup; proven on crypto 1h for catching multi-hour momentum legs without chasing single-bar noise.
strategies.notes: Starter config. Backtest before going live; MACD params can be tuned tighter on 1h crypto (e.g. 8,21,5) for more trades.

strategies.label: BTC trend-follow — SMA crossover 20/50 on 1h
strategies.description: Classic SMA crossover: fast crosses above slow = long, cross down = exit. 20/50 on 1h captures swing trends without the whipsaw of faster periods on crypto noise.
strategies.notes: Tune window_fast up (→30) and window_slow up (→100) if churn is high. ADX filter can be added to trade only in trending markets.

strategies.label: ETH mean-reversion — RSI oversold bounce on 4h
strategies.description: Enters when RSI crosses up through 30 (oversold exit), exits when RSI crosses down through 70 (overbought exit). 4h timeframe keeps the setup away from intraday noise.
strategies.notes: Classic RSI(14, 30/70). Tighter thresholds (25/75) yield fewer but higher-quality entries on ranging markets.

strategies.label: BTC momentum + trend filter — MACD entry, SMA filter on 1h
strategies.description: Enters on MACD bullish cross ONLY when price is above SMA(50) — filters out counter-trend momentum pops. Exits on MACD bearish cross.
strategies.notes: The trend filter cuts trade count ~60% but typically lifts win rate. Good starter for a cautious momentum player.

strategies.label: ETH trend-follow — EMA crossover with ADX filter on 4h
strategies.description: EMA(12) crosses EMA(26) up = long, cross down = exit. ADX > 25 filter ensures we only trade when there's actual trend strength.
strategies.notes: ADX filter is the single biggest quality lever on crypto trend systems. Disable it to see the noise for yourself.

strategies.label: BTC momentum long-term — MACD on 1d
strategies.description: Daily MACD(12,26,9) — the textbook swing-trading setup. Low trade count but high per-trade expectancy on BTC historically.
strategies.notes: 1d MACD expects 4-8 trades per year on BTC. Use for a low-attention, high-conviction live wallet.

strategies.label: ETH momentum — ROC positive + above SMA on 4h
strategies.description: Enters when Rate of Change flips positive AND price is above SMA(50). Exits on ROC flipping negative. Catches sustained momentum rather than single-bar spikes.
strategies.notes: ROC period 12 = ~2 days on 4h. Shorter periods = more trades but more noise; 6 is the typical noise floor.

strategies.label: BTC trend + volume confirmation — EMA cross + PVO on 1h
strategies.description: EMA(9) crosses EMA(21) up AND PVO (Percentage Volume Oscillator) is bullish = enter. Combines price trend + volume participation — reduces false breakouts.
strategies.notes: PVO filter rejects ~40% of pure price-only crossovers. Good defense against choppy ranges.

strategies.label: ETH mean-reversion tight — Stoch oversold bounce on 1h
strategies.description: Stochastic(14,3) oversold (< 20) crosses back up = enter. Exits on overbought (> 80). Tighter + faster than RSI mean-reversion.
strategies.notes: Dual oscillator confluence. High trade count, small edges — slippage/fees matter more here. Measure carefully in backtest.

strategies.label: BTC breakout — Ichimoku TK cross on 4h
strategies.description: Ichimoku Tenkan/Kijun crossover — a trend-confirmation breakout. Enters on TK cross up with bullish cloud posture, exits on TK cross down.
strategies.notes: Ichimoku is self-filtering — the bullish-cloud filter ensures we only take TK crosses in confirmed uptrends. Classic 9/26/52 periods.

strategies.label: ETH trend-follow conservative — SMA 50/200 on 1d
strategies.description: The 'golden cross' on daily: SMA(50) crosses above SMA(200). Long-term position trading. Trade count per year: 1-3.
strategies.notes: Slow as molasses, but on BTC/ETH historically captures the bulk of bull markets. Zero daily attention required.

strategies.label: BTC volatility expansion — ATR filter + MACD on 4h
strategies.description: Trade MACD crossovers only during high-volatility regimes (ATR-based). Avoids the death of trend systems during ranging markets.
strategies.notes: Volatility filter reduces trade count but systematically skips the worst regime for trend systems. Good pairing for momentum goals.
